description: Clean set of buyer intent/type categories the copilot detects from a message, merged from all books' Section-10 personas and buyer_psychology (including Never Split the Difference's three buyer styles + three-voice model, pulled from its plays/psychology since its classifiers_personas was empty). Each category lists concrete observable signals, the likely underlying interest, the recommended PRIN ids to deploy, risk flags, and source books. NOTE: several signals (e.g. like-counts, days-on-market, repeat-buyer status, off-platform payment patterns) are marketplace-data features the books do not supply — flagged in gaps.

intents.label: Serious / interest-driven buyer
intents.description: Engaged, responsive buyer asking genuine questions, stating a real budget or use, ready to move to purchase. The highest-conversion intent — don't over-negotiate.
observable_signals: asks specific questions (measurements, condition, ship time)
states a real budget or 'is this for me/a gift'
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Wants this specific item at a fair price and to buy with confidence (security + economic well-being).
risk_flags: over-negotiating a ready yes and souring it
missing the close by stalling on more questions

intents.label: Lowballer
intents.description: Opens with an offer far below ask, often with little engagement; may be a bargain-hunter, a competer, or a value-claimer testing how far you'll drop.
observable_signals: '$X take it or leave it' with no reasoning
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Could be a hard budget cap, habitual haggling for bragging rights, or a value-claimer probing your floor — decode before reacting.
risk_flags: caving to the anchor and signalling lots of room
splitting the difference reflexively
over-investing effort on a non-serious bargain-hunter

intents.label: Anchor-shopper / comparison buyer
intents.description: Justifies a low price by citing a cheaper alternative ('I've seen it for $25', 'another seller is cheaper'); may be a real comparison or a bluff.
observable_signals: 'I can get this cheaper elsewhere / on another account'
cites a competitor price or sold listing
asks you to match a price
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Wants reassurance they're not overpaying; may be over-estimating their alternative (the cheaper one may be worse condition/unauthenticated/slow).
risk_flags: dropping price to a fabricated or non-comparable comp
disparaging the competitor instead of differentiating
matching a bluff

intents.label: Hesitant / uncertain buyer
intents.description: Wants the item but isn't sure — about fit, condition, authenticity, or budget; a nervous or first-time secondhand buyer. The interest is usually NOT price.
observable_signals: '$40 feels like a lot for something used'
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Value-doubt or fit/condition fear, not price — needs proof and confidence, not a discount.
risk_flags: dropping price when proof would have closed it
pressuring into reactance
skipping the small-yes ladder

intents.label: Ghoster / staller / fence-sitter
intents.description: Went quiet after engaging, or keeps saying 'maybe later / next payday' without committing; saved or liked but won't message or pay.
observable_signals: silence 24h+ after a question or offer
committed verbally then went silent before paying
intents.likely_underlying_interest: An unmet blocker (price, fit, trust, timing) or simple inertia; may be conflict-averse (avoider) who flees pressure.
risk_flags: nagging into reactance
fabricating scarcity to force a move
no commitment device so the verbal yes evaporates

intents.label: Bundler / collaborator
intents.description: Open and exploratory, interested in multiple items, asks 'can we make this work?' — the highest-value persona for integrative deals.
observable_signals: interested in / has liked multiple items
'is there a way we can work something out?'
receptive to bundles, open about needs
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Wants overall value and a fair package; happy to grow the deal if it benefits both.
risk_flags: giving away margin by stacking free concessions
complicating a buyer who actually wants just one item

intents.label: Rude / aggressive buyer
intents.description: Uses insults, mockery, or hostility ('is this a joke', 'total ripoff'); may be a competer being blunt, or an attacker trying to throw you off balance.
observable_signals: insults / mocking ('is this a joke', 'overpriced garbage')
attacks on the item or your credibility
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Often fear (of being ripped off) or a tactic to rattle you into a concession; the power comes from your reaction.
risk_flags: counter-punching (tanks the deal and your liking)
caving to stop the discomfort
escalating an ambiguous-terse message you misread

intents.label: Bargaining bully / extortionist
intents.description: Treats everything as principle, uses threats (bad reviews, disputes) and serial nibbling as tactics, never reciprocates. A persona, not a style — respond on leverage, route real threats to safety.
observable_signals: repeated escalation when pushed back on
intents.likely_underlying_interest: To grind you down and extract more than fair; respects only strength.
risk_flags: rewarding the threat with a concession (invites more)
matching abuse / retaliating
not documenting the thread before reporting

intents.label: Scam / off-platform actor
intents.description: Pushes to leave the platform or sets up a payment scam — off-platform pay, overpayment/refund, fake payment screenshot, ship-before-pay, chargeback bait. SAFETY STOP, not a negotiation.
observable_signals: 'pay outside the app / Venmo F&F / this link'
'ship it first and I'll pay you'
sends a 'payment confirmation' screenshot before you see funds
intents.likely_underlying_interest: To remove buyer/seller protections so they can take the item or money — not a genuine buyer.
risk_flags: HARD STOP: never negotiate, never apply balcony/bridge logic
never ship before confirmed on-platform payment
report and disengage if pressed

intents.label: 'Is it available?' low-signal pinger
intents.description: Sends a one-line availability check with no other engagement. A weak buying signal — convert gently, don't over-negotiate.
observable_signals: 'is this still available?' and nothing else
no number, no questions, no context
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Mild interest / casual browsing; possibly a buying signal that needs a nudge to become real.
risk_flags: a dead 'yes' that kills momentum
launching a hard pitch off a one-liner

intents.label: Relationship / loyal / repeat buyer
intents.description: Warm, polite, complimentary; a repeat buyer, follower, or local who values your service and the item's story. Accommodate to keep the relationship.
observable_signals: warm, complimentary tone ('I love this!', 'totally fine if not')
cares about the item's story or your service
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Ongoing relationship and feeling valued; a small genuine concession buys big goodwill and repeat business.
risk_flags: over-charging and losing a repeat customer
treating warmth as weakness
letting an accommodator over-defer into a regretted sale

intents.label: Skeptic / authenticity-wary buyer
intents.description: Fixated on legitimacy and risk ('is this legit?', 'how do I know it's real?', 'don't want to get scammed'); aggression here usually springs from fear.
observable_signals: asks about your sales history / reviews
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Security — fear of buying a fake or being defrauded; wants proof and a safety net.
risk_flags: bluffing expertise/authentication you lack
getting defensive instead of providing proof
reading their fear as an attack

intents.label: Complainer / post-sale dispute
intents.description: Already bought and something went wrong — not as described, damaged, late, wrong item. Acknowledge feelings, own real fault, resolve with options. Distinct from pre-sale haggling.
observable_signals: 'this isn't the color in the photos'
loaded blame ('this is your fault', 'you didn't mention X')
intents.likely_underlying_interest: To feel heard and made whole; behind the anger is often fear of losing money or being dismissed.
risk_flags: jumping to a fix before acknowledging the feeling
defending good intentions before owning the harm
over-conceding (panic refund) or stonewalling on policy

intents.label: Ambiguous-terse message (unreadable tone)
intents.description: Two-word or cryptic message ('k.', 'wow.', 'seriously?') with no context — tone unreadable in text; do not assume hostility.
observable_signals: all-caps or no punctuation that could read several ways
intents.likely_underlying_interest: Unknown — could be anger, sarcasm, excitement, or nothing about you; needs a neutral clarifier.
risk_flags: over-reacting to a misread negative tone
firing an irreversible defensive reply
